title: docmend manifest record (DR-004)
description: One line of the append-only JSON Lines manifest written during apply (FR-006). This schema validates a SINGLE record — the manifest file is NDJSON because a single JSON document cannot be appended crash-safely (adr-0005/IR-007); each mutation appends (and fsyncs) exactly one record BEFORE the run can be considered to have recorded it, incrementally, never only at run end (spec 12.3). Records carry everything needed to mechanically restore the pre-apply state (IR-008, adr-0004). Pinned at MS-1 per adr-0005; implemented by the MS-3 writer, which may extend it with MINOR bumps.

docmend_id.description: Stable document identity (UUIDv7, adr-0008); `docmend restore` replays records per docmend.id in LIFO order (IR-008).

seq.description: Monotonic record number within this run's manifest, starting at 1 — establishes the LIFO replay order and makes truncation detectable.

operation.description: What mutated: 'rename' moved the file without content change; 'rewrite' replaced content in place; 'rename_and_rewrite' did both as one atomic action.

backup_path.description: Verified backup copy location when tool-written backups were enabled (FR-006); null under the Git/external/low-risk-opt-in preservation strategies.

after_sha256.description: Hash of the file as written; equals before_sha256 for a pure rename. Null when result is 'failed' (the original is intact, spec 10.4).
